observations.recommendation:
1.13. We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:
a. instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal General Services
Officer/ Property Custodian to conduct yearly physical count of the local
road network projects and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
the Road Network System and submit the same to the audit team;
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to compute separately the depreciation
for the road components based on their respective cost and useful life;
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide required disclosures in the
Notes to Financial Statements in the format required by the COA Circular
No. 2015-008;
d. instruct the General Service Officer (GSO) and Municipal Accountant to
prepare and maintain LRNPC and LRNPC, respectively, and ensure that
records are religiously reconciled; and
e. instruct the GSO to prepare and maintain The Local Road Inventory and
Road Map and reconcile it with those recorded in the Registry of Public
Infrastructure.

2.22. In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended and Management
agreed to:
a. direct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete annual physical
inventory of PPE and ensure the reconciliation of the accounting
records with the results of inventory-taking per RPCPPE;
b. require the Property Custodian to maintain an updated Property
Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) and ensure that transfer of custody
of assets from one person to another be properly documented.
Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall maintain an updated PPE
Ledger Cards to facilitate the accuracy and reliability of PPE accounts
and the Property Custodian shall ensure that all properties are tagged
with property stickers indicating the vital information required by rules
and regulations;
c. direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the reported tangible
items totaling ₱2.909 million detailed in Annex B from their
corresponding PPE accounts to the correct semi-expendable property
accounts. Henceforth, ensure the proper classification and monitoring
of agency’s tangible assets as prescribed under COA Circular No.
2022-004; and
d. create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to fast track the appraisal
of the unserviceable properties totaling P1.644 million and dispose the
same to avoid further deterioration of the said assets in accordance
with to Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445.

3.8. We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Assessor to submit to the audit team copies of OCT
and tax declaration of all land owned by the Municipality for verification
and conduct assessment of these properties to come up with the
revalued amounts;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile with the Municipal Assessor
and adjust balances to reflect the revalued amounts of the properties
after the conduct of assessment; and
c. Acquire land title for the properties with fair market value totaling ₱16.308
million in compliance with Section 23 (2) Title I-B, Book, 1987 of the
Administrative Code.

4.6. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to compute for the
depreciation of the above PPE using the estimated useful life under COA
Circular No. 2003-007. The computed depreciation for prior years shall be
recorded as prior year adjustments.

5.5. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the effective and efficient
monitoring of the RPT and SET Receivables account balances and submit
the same to the audit team within the required timeline. Furthermore, we
recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare the List of
Delinquent Taxes for CY 2024 and years thereafter and henceforth, submit
the same to the audit team.

6.6. We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in tracing the details of
said dormant payables and initiate the reconciliation of accounts with the
concerned NGAs and LGUs to confirm if a reciprocal Due from account
is recorded in their books; and
b. submit to the audit team and to the concerned NGAs and LGUs the
supporting documents for post-audit and liquidation of the fund transfer.
Otherwise, the unliquidated amount of P2.992 million shall be refunded
to the source agencies or to the Bureau of Treasury.

7.8. We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to intensify
their efforts to establish the existence and validity and likewise collect
said receivables, respectively; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct an assessment of the
collectability of the dormant receivable accounts and request write-off
according to the procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016 and henceforth, prepare and maintain
subsidiary ledgers for all receivables in accordance with Section 4(e) of
the MNGAS for LGUs.

8.6. We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:
a. direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the
Certificate of Completion of the completed projects amounting to ₱1.640
million;
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal
Engineer to determine the status of the Buildings and Other Structures
totaling P447,298.01; and
c. direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entry of
the completed projects to their appropriate Public Infrastructure
accounts, and submit the necessary documents to the Audit Team to
serve as basis for the request for inspection and technical evaluation.

9.6. We recommended and Management agreed to direct both the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records and effect
necessary corrections. Furthermore, we recommend the immediate
submission to the audit team of the liquidations for the fund transfers
totaling ₱0.919 million.

10.15. In view of the above noted lapses, we recommended and Management
agreed to:
a. direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately the accumulated
undeposited collections totaling ₱184,426.61 in the General Fund and
forfeit the over-deposit of ₱31,366.17 in favor of the government and
recognize a Miscellaneous Income if reason of overage cannot be
satisfactorily traced and justified. We also recommend that the Municipal
Treasurer, henceforth, ensure that all collections be deposited intact and
in required frequency as mandated by Item 33, Chapter 2 of the Revised
Cash Examination Manual to prevent the exposure of agency funds to
possible loss and misappropriation;
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Cash in Treasury
account with the records of the Municipal Treasurer and trace the noted
over/understatements and effect necessary adjusting entries. We further
recommend the Municipal Accountant to henceforth, prepare and submit
to the audit team quarterly reconciliation of the Cash in Local Treasury
account;
c. instruct the Collecting Officers and/or Field collectors to henceforth remit
their collections to the Municipal Treasurer immediately to avoid risk of
loss and misappropriations. We also recommend the Municipal
Treasurer to monitor the remittances of the collectors.

11.6. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Office of the
Auditor all financial accounts and reports within the first 10 days of the
ensuing month.

12.7. We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following proposed
adjusting journal entry to recognize the shares of the DPWH and OBO of
the Building Permit Fees for CY 2024:
Account Debit Credit
Government Equity 1,737.00
Trust Liabilities 1,302.75
Due to NGAs 434.25
b. create and maintain a Trust Fund account for the building permit shares
in compliance with Item 2.1 of the DILG and DPWH Joint Memorandum
Circular (JMC) No. 001 dated July 4, 2013; and
c. direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the share of DPWH amounting to
₱1,518.21 for CYs 2023 and 2024

13.5. We recommended and Management agreed to demand from the concerned
barangays the immediate submission of the liquidation reports for the
outstanding fund transfers of ₱4.59 million through the issuance of follow-
up letters and/or demand letters. Moreover, if liquidation reports remain
unsubmitted after reasonable follow up and demands, we recommended and
management agreed to demand a refund from all concerned barangays in
compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

14.6. We recommended and Management agreed to submit the properly
accomplished and duly signed DTRs and ARs of the job orders workers
listed in the Annex B of this report and henceforth, ensure that paid payrolls
are duly supported with complete and properly signed and accomplished
supporting documents as required by pertinent provisions.

15.4. We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Henceforth, submit list of all on-going government projects/ programs/
activities (PPAs) and those that were implemented at the beginning of the
year; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to the audit team within 10 days after the
award of the infrastructure projects or before the start of the PPAs that
the appropriate projects signboards and /or public notices are already
posted at the project site, for proper validation.

16.5. We recommended and Management agreed to submit contract documents
for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects listed in item 16.1
henceforth, submit copies of duly perfected contracts within five working
days from perfection thereof to the Auditor for prompt review and evaluation
as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

17.7. We recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure
preparation of AIP and submission of the same to the audit team.
Furthermore, we recommended and management agreed to henceforth,
prioritize the implementation of projects that are in the AIP.

18.6. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer
to request the transfer of the unexpended DRRMF for the current year
totaling ₱1.782 million from GF to the depository account for Trust Liability-
DRRM fund and henceforth, comply with the regulations on the transfer of
unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund.
Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
henceforth, prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for the monitoring
of the LDRRM fund and account and submit the same to the audit team for
verification.

19.3. We commended management on their compliance in allotting at least five
percent of the total IRA for the year and for prioritizing the implementation of
PPAs that were within the agency’s GPB.

20.6. We recommended and Management agreed to strictly implement the timely
filing of tax returns as per BIR RR No. 1-2013 dated January 23, 2013 and
comply with and rules on remittance of taxes withheld from employees and
government suppliers as prescribed under DOF-DBM-COA Joint Circular No.
1-2000 dated January 3, 2000 and Joint Circular No. 1-2000A dated July 31,
2001.

21.7. We reiterated our previous recommendation and both the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to exert effort to reconcile
their record with the GSIS. Henceforth, regularly reconcile their quarterly
financial records with the records of other government agencies.

22.5. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to remit to Pag-IBIG the unremitted balance of ₱1,060.80 and
henceforth, ensure that withholdings of employees’ and employers’
contributions be remitted intact and within the prescribed period of the RA
No. 9679.

23.4. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to remit to PhilHealth the unremitted balance of ₱166,409.32
immediately and henceforth, ensure that withholdings of employees’ and

Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in the preparation and

and environmental outcomes;
22.8.3. instruct the Municipal Engineer to consult the duly prepared
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for infrastructure projects if
any (for unimplemented projects), since said report include the
timeline for the implementation of the project. Justification as to
the reason for the non-implementation should also be sought to
the responsible persons; and,
22.8.4. instruct the BAC/Municipal Accountant/Treasurer to
immediately submit contract documents and other necessary
documents for proper review and evaluation of the said projects
funded under the 20% Development Fund.

through the OCD on or before the 5th day following the end of

23.7.2. Sees to it that in cases of lump sum appropriations containing
general terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance/
resolution must always be secured by the Municipal Mayor to
serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the
Municipal Government in accordance with Section 22(c) of R.A.
No. 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.
23.7.3. Submit the bidding and other essential documents to support
validity of the purchase of mass transport vehicle.

shall be properly supported with RIS and listings of

23.7.5. Strictly comply with the relevant provisions with regard to the
utilization of the LDRRM funds. Personal services, maintenance
and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the
LDRRMO as well as the regular MOOE and capital outlay of
the municipality shall be sourced from the General Fund. The
LDRRMF shall be strictly utilized for the
implementation of DRRM programs.
23.7.6. Submit the necessary documents for proper review and
evaluation of the said projects funded under LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Engineer establish the existence and validity of
the Construction in Progress – Infrastructure Asset accounts, and furnish to
COA a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the
foregoing CIP balances. The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with
the Municipal Engineer to determine the status of these projects, and analyze
the CIP accounts, prepare the adjusting entry or closing entry of the completed
projects to their appropriate Public Infrastructure accounts, and submit the
necessary documents to COA to serve as basis for the request for inspection
and technical evaluation. The accounting unit should maintain complete and
updated subsidiary ledgers for CIP and Public Infrastructure accounts as
prescribed under PPSAS and keep tract on the status of infrastructure
projects implemented by the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Engineer submit all the supporting documents to prove that the project has
been completed for proper presentation in the financial statements, and
request for the inspection of the said projects.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management ensure compliance
with the conduct of inventory and the recognition of Road Networks in the
books of accounts equivalent to the required transition period, as well as the
disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2015-008. We have also reiterated our recommendation that the
Municipal Accountant submit the JEV and its supporting documents as basis
for the derecognition of the San Miguel Multi-purpose Drying Pavement.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary
adjusting entries, take extra-care in posting and recording transactions and
adhere to prescribed accounting regulations to present fairly the financial
statements.

We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Engineer submit the approved program of work and other supporting
documents for the project “Rehabilitation and Maintenance of Barangay
Roads” for proper evaluation and classification of accounts. Also, we
recommend that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement
vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation and that no
disbursement should be paid unless supported with complete documentation.

We have recommended the following:
5.8.1 That the Disbursing Officer/Accountable Officers regularly submit the
Report of Disbursements/Liquidation documents to the Accounting
Office for timely recording in the books of accounts for fair presentation
of the financial statements. Likewise, refund any unexpended balance of
cash advance at the end of the year as prescribed under regulations;
5.8.2 That the Municipal Accountant require the Disbursing
Officer/Accountable Officers to submit promptly the liquidation
documents and shall record the liquidations on time to ensure that all
transactions are included in the financial statements. Also, record
liquidation only when properly supported with liquidation report and
supporting documents; and
5.8.3 No cash advance should be granted to an accountable officer unless the
previous cash advance given to him has been fully liquidated.

We have recommended the following:
6.11.1 The Municipal Treasurer properly verify Reports of Collections and
Deposits. He shall check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to
initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on
hand and make a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the
custody of the collectors and check against ending balances on hand
column. Accountable forms shall be issued only to those accountable
officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections.
6.11.2 The Municipal Treasurer present all the unaccounted accountable
forms and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for verification
and come up with the audited Cash – Local Treasury balance.
6.11.3 The Municipal Accountant install and maintain an effective internal
control system and internal audit system on collections and deposits in
order to safeguard and prevent possible loss of the accountable forms
and government funds of the Municipality.
6.11.4 Valid justifications be submitted to explain the deficiencies noted on
collections and the unaccounted/ missing accountable forms.

We have recommended that management submit the Official Receipts/Sales
Invoices for audit purposes. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant require payees to issue corresponding official receipts
or sales invoices (for goods) and sign the receive portion of the DVs for best
interest of the municipality and pursuant to COA Circular No. 2004-006 and
Section 237 of the NIRC and RMC No. 2-2014.

We have recommended that fully accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets be
prepared to ensure proper use of government vehicle and consumption of fuel,
summary of which shall be prepared at the end of each month in a Monthly
Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption and submit to this
Office for audit/evaluation.

We have recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions
of the OEC particularly on the disbursements of public funds for public work
project during the 45 day period before regular election to avoid possible
imposition of sanctions or penalties by COMELEC as provided for in Section
264 of the OEC.

We have recommended that Management require the concerned personnel to
submit the required supporting documents COA for audit purposes and
direct the concerned department head/personnel to submit justification for
the noted lapses. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that
succeeding transactions be properly supported with complete documentation
and in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations in order to
establish the validity and propriety of the transactions and disbursements.
Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented shall be
discontinued.

We have recommended that Management use DPWH Department
Order No. 197, series of 2016 or its updated issuances on the matter in its
succeeding computation of the ABC of infrastructure projects for CY 2018
onwards.

We have recommended that the BAC invite observers in writing in all
stages of the procurement process in accordance with the provisions of
the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
submit written justification/s on the above-noted finding.

We have recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the
procedures on receipts, opening and preliminary examination of bids as
provided for in the Procurement Manual for LGUs and the 2016 Revised
IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that the BAC ensure strict compliance with the
requirements of Sections 25 and 27 of the IRR of RA 9184 in relation to
Section XI of Appendix 10 on the use of Bid Securing Declaration. Henceforth, succeeding BSD be in its standard form and duly notarized.

We have recommended that the BAC submit written justification/s on
the above-noted findings. Henceforth, ensure that prospective bidders
submit complete eligibility requirements before they are marked as
"passed" and declared as LCB.

We have recommended that the BAC and TWG submit written
justification/s on the above-noted findings. Henceforth, notice and
execution of award to the bidder-contractor should only be done after
the conduct of the post-qualification process.

We have recommended that management see to it that future winning
bidders comply with all the requirements set forth under the 2016
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 such as the posting of Performance
Bond before the awarding of contract and contract signing.

We have recommended that the Municipal Government, through the
BAC Secretariat comply with the posting requirements on all its
procurement. Notices of Award and Proceed, and the approved
contract be posted in the PhilGEPS website and website of the
municipality, if any, within the reglementary period as required by
the 2017 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that the BAC submit written justification on
the above-noted finding.

We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses especially
on the splitting of procurement. Hence, all procurements of the municipality
should be conducted through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No.
9184 and its IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI
of the IRR as recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the head of
the procuring entity. Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to
only in highly exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184. In all
instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price
for the LGU is obtained.

We have recommended for the submission of the written request by the
contractor for the release of advance payment and copy of the irrevocable
standby letter of credit, bank guarantee and/or surety bond, issued by a
surety or insurance company and confirmed by the municipality, as substitute
for the release of the advance payment. If no such copy is available, submit
justification why no substitute guaranty was required. Henceforth, require
the posting of irrevocable standby letter of credit, bank guarantee or surety
bond in favor of the Government as substitute for the release of the advance
payment.

We have recommended that management submit the above-enumerated
documents for proper review and evaluation. Likewise, submit written
justifications on the deficiencies noted.

We have recommended that management justify the above noted findings.
We also recommend for the submission of irrevocable standby letters of
credit, bank guarantees and/or surety bonds as substitute for the release of
retention moneys. Henceforth, impose the ten percent (10%) retention on
progress payments and require the posting of irrevocable standby letters of
credit, bank guarantees or surety bonds for a valid duration in favor of the
Government as substitute for the release thereof.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-
charge with release of contracts/ purchase orders to furnish the auditor with
copies of these documents together with the required attachments/supporting
papers within five (5) days after perfection thereof and ensure that the same
are in the standard form as prescribed by GPPB and as required by COA
Circular No. 2009-001 for auditorial and technical review.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management prepare
its APP and supporting PPMPs to serve as guide in the procurement of goods
and infrastructure projects and furnish COA of the same for audit/evaluation
purposes. No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with
the approved APP of the procuring entity.

18.7.1 We have recommended that management, in cases of lump sum
appropriations containing general terms, seek a separate SB
appropriation ordinance/resolution to serve as an authority to enter
into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance to
Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.

18.11.1 We have recommended that Management carefully prepare the 20%
Development Fund Investment Plan. Utilize the fund appropriated
for every programs, projects and activities (PPAs) strictly in
accordance with the plan and budget to avoid over and under
expenditure and partial/non-implementation of PPAs. Expenditures
made should not exceed the appropriated amount for every PPA.

18.16.1 We have recommended that management justify the above-noted
lapses. Utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be limited
on programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the
attainment of socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or
capital expenditures that will yield greater benefit to the people in
accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1
as updated under DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1.

18.21.1 We have recommended that management immediately submit the
above-enumerated documents for proper review and evaluation of the
said projects funded under the 20% Development Fund. We have
likewise reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the
municipality adhere to the complete documentary requirements for
equipment rental as provided by COA Circular No. 2012-001.

18.24.1 We have recommended that the municipality utilize the unexpended
balance of allocated funds for the intended purposes after due evaluation
whether these are still priority concerns taking into consideration
current developments and to accordingly re-appropriate the funds of
PPA which have become irrelevant or not responsive to current priority
undertakings.

19.6.1 We have reiterated our recommendation that management submit
monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF sourced
from GAA and Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash
Donations of the municipality to NDRRMC through the OCD on or
before the 5th day following the end of each month and furnish copy to
the audit team for audit/evaluation.

19.9.1 We have recommended that the Management implement proper
monitoring and control on cash donations received for DRRM.
Henceforth, ensure that CDUR are attached to the DV before the
utilization of cash donation for DRRM are made. Also, the Accounting
Unit prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledger (SL) for "Trust
Liabilities-DRRMF" for cash donations received amounting to
₱100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while those below
₱100,000.00 shall be grouped and posted in one SL labeled as
"Various Donors" per purpose.

19.10.1 We have recommended that management sees to it that in cases of
lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB
appropriation ordinance/ resolution must always be secured by the
Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in
behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance with Section 22(c)
of R.A. No. 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.

19.19.1 We have recommended that the Municipal Government utilize funds
for the specific purpose it was created for in consonance with Section
336 of R.A. No. 7160 to prevent charges of technical malversation.

19.24.1 We have recommended that management strictly comply with the
provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and the related subsequent provisions on
LDRRMF utilization. Personal services, maintenance and other
operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRMO as well as
the regular MOOE and capital outlay of the municipality shall be
sourced from the General Fund. The LDRRMF shall be strictly
utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs.

19.30.1 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up under
the appropriate expense account the inventory items upon
distribution to end-users/beneficiaries; see to it that these are
properly supported with RIS and listings of recipients/beneficiaries
(with their address and contact number for audit purposes) duly
signed/acknowledged, showing the items and quantity of items
distributed, the ARE for issuance of PPE to COA for evaluation; and
take up the necessary adjusting entry for the small items erroneously
taken up as PPE and issuances thereof shall be supported with ICS.

19.35.1 We have recommended that management submit the above-
enumerated documents for proper review and evaluation of the said
projects funded under LDRRMF. We have likewise reiterated our
prior years’ recommendation that the municipality adhere to the
complete documentary requirements for equipment rental as
provided by COA Circular No. 2012-001.

19.41 We have recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan Members pass a board
resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to
open a separate trust fund bank account for the LDRRMF. Furthermore,
the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account and a complete set of
subsidiary for the said fund. Likewise, effect the necessary adjusting
entries upon transfer to present fairly the respective account balances in the
financial statements.

20.6 In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that:
20.6.1 the appropriation in the SEF Local School Board Budget and/or
Resolution expressed in generic terms be supported with detail as to
the projects and activities and cost to be funded by SEF for proper
evaluation and monitoring of fund utilization. Henceforth, ensure that
Special Education Funds be spent specifically in fulfillment of the
purpose for which the fund was created as determined and approved
by the Local School Board;
20.6.2 the above-enumerated lacking documents be submitted for proper
review and evaluation of the said projects funded under SEF; and
20.6.3 the prescribed rules and regulations on processing of disbursements be
strictly observed, Henceforth, ensure that all disbursements are
properly supported with complete documentation before payments are
made.

21.23 We have recommended that the Municipal Government:
21.23.1 Submit GPB to the PPDO of the Provincial Government to ensure the
alignment of the municipal GAD PPAs with the priorities of the
province, then from PPDO to the DILG Regional Offices for review
and approval.
21.23.2 Identify GAD PPAs through the use of HGDG which would be basis
in the budget determination depending on its gender responsiveness
or attribute to GAD interventions.
21.23.3 Accompany GAD ARs with the required reports/documents be
furnished to COA to facilitate audit of GAD Fund utilization.
21.23.4 The Municipal Government utilize funds for the specific purpose it
was created for in consonance with Section 336 of R.A. No. 7160 to
prevent charges of technical malversation.
21.23.5 Establish and/or strengthen their monitoring and evaluation systems
to assess the effectiveness and efficiency of their gender
mainstreaming efforts, and the M&E activities shall be included in the
annual GPB.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit all taxes withheld within
the time prescribed as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid
possible imposition of sanctions by the BIR and for additional income of the
government.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding internal revenue
taxes as mandated by laws, rules and regulations on all its future purchases
of goods and payments for services for additional income of the government
and to avoid possible imposition of sanctions by the BIR.

We have also recommended that Management justify the noted lapses particularly on the questionable official receipts for the disbursement on fuel consumption.

We have recommended that management submit the legal basis authorizing payment of “Communication Allowance” to the various Officers of the municipality.  If not, then liquidation documents/official receipts for the telephone expenses should be submitted to this Office for audit/evaluation.  Government officials and employees should only be entitled to allowances, incentives and other benefits expressly provided by law, and other statutory authority, and the rules and regulations promulgated by competent authority.
Management comment:
Seven (7) officers of the municipality already submitted their official receipts for the telephone expenses.  Management said during the conduct of exit conference that the release of communication expenses for those officers with unsubmitted official receipts were suspended for CY 2017.

We have recommended that management submit the above-enumerated documents for proper review and evaluation.  Likewise, submit written justifications why the project remained uncompleted as of December 31, 2016.

We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses especially on the splitting of procurement.  Hence, all procurements of the municipality should be conducted through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Section 52 of the IRR as recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the head of the procuring entity.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184.  In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

We have recommended that management see to it that future winning bidders have complied with all the requirements set forth under the Revised IRR such as the posting of Performance Bond before the awarding of contract and contract signing.  We also recommended for the submission of the performance security for the above-stated contract of infrastructure projects for audit/evaluation.  Henceforth, the BAC ensure that the municipality strictly abide by the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations on procurement.

We have recommended that the members of the BAC explain the noted lapses.  Henceforth, strictly comply with the mandatory periods of action on its procurement activities so as not to affect the validity of the bidding and procurement process of the municipality.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management prepare its APP and supporting PPMPs to serve as guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and furnish COA of the same for audit/evaluation purposes.  No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP of the procuring entity.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-charged with release of contracts/purchase orders to furnish the auditor with copies of these documents together with the required attachments/supporting papers within five (5) days after perfection thereof and ensure that the same are in the standard form as prescribed by GPPB and as required by COA Circular No. 2009-001 for auditorial and technical review.

We have recommended that management justify the above noted findings.  We have also recommended for the submission of irrevocable standby letters of credit, bank guarantees and/or surety bonds as substitute for the release of retention moneys.  Henceforth, impose the ten percent (10%) retention on progress payments and require the posting of irrevocable standby letters of credit, bank guarantees or surety bonds for a valid duration in favor of the Government as substitute for the release thereof.

We have recommended for the submission of a copy of the irrevocable standby letter of credit, bank guarantee and/or surety bonds as substitute for the release of the advance payment, if any.  If no such copy is available, submit justification why no substitute guaranty was required.    Henceforth, impose the ten percent (10%) retention on progress payments and require the posting of irrevocable standby letter of credit, bank guarantee or surety bond in favor of the Government as substitute for the release thereof.  We also recommend for the presentation of the original copy of the Official Receipt.

We have recommended that management, in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, seek a separate SB appropriation ordinance/resolution to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance to Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.

We have recommended that management carefully prepare the 20% Development Fund Investment Plan.  Utilize the fund appropriated for every programs, projects and activities (PPAs) strictly in accordance with the plan and budget to avoid over and under expenditure and partial/non-implementation of PPAs.  Expenditures made should not exceed the appropriated amount for every PPA.

We have recommended that management justify the above-noted lapses.  Utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be limited on programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will yield greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We have recommended that management immediately submit the above-enumerated documents for proper review and evaluation of the said projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.  We have likewise reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the municipality adhere to the complete documentary requirements for equipment rental as provided by COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We have recommended that management submit monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF sourced from GAA and Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations of the municipality to NDRRMC through the OCD on or before the 5th day following the end of each month, and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Mayor ensure that CDUR are attached to the DV before the utilization of cash donation for DRRM for proper monitoring and control.  Also, the Accounting Unit shall prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledger (SL) for "Trust Liabilities-DRRMF" for cash donations received amounting to ₱100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while those below ₱100,000.00 shall be grouped and posted in one SL labeled as "Various Donors" per purpose.

We have recommended that management sees to it that in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance/ resolution must always be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance to Section 22(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.

We have recommended that management justify the noted findings.  Henceforth, ensure the existence of an appropriation in accordance with Section 7 of R.A. No. 9184, Section 85(1) of P.D. No. 1445, Section 305 of R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence, before transactions are made or contracts are entered into by the municipality.  Fund availability is, as it has always been, an indispensable prerequisite to the execution of any government contract involving the expenditure of public funds by all government agencies at all levels.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and the related subsequent provisions on LDRRMF utilization.   Personal services, maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRMO as well as the regular MOOE and capital outlay of the municipality shall be sourced from the General Fund.  The LDRRMF shall be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs.

We have recommended the following:
that the Municipal Accountant take up under the appropriate expense account the inventory items upon distribution to end-users/beneficiaries;
see to it that these are properly supported with RIS and listings of recipients/beneficiaries which are duly signed acknowledging receipt of the items and quantities actually received.
that the Municipal Accountant submit the RIS and the list of recipients/beneficiaries (with their address and contact number for audit purposes) duly signed/acknowledged, showing the items and quantity of items distributed, the ARE for issuance of PPE to COA for evaluation; and
take up the necessary adjusting entry for the small items erroneously taken up as PPE and issuances thereof shall be supported with ICS.
Management comment:
During the exit conference, management said that they are already seeking the signatures of the recipients/beneficiaries for submission.

We have recommended for the submission of the above-enumerated documents for proper review and evaluation of the said projects funded under LDRRMF.  We have likewise reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the municipality adhere to the complete documentary requirements for equipment rental as provided by COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We have recommended that management justify for the release and use of the QRF before the declaration of the state of calamity.  Henceforth, ensure that the release and use of the QRF shall be supported with Sanggunian Resolution and the declaration of the state of calamity.

We have recommended that management comply with the regulations on the transfer of unexpended/ unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund.  We have recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan Members pass a board resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate trust fund bank account for the LDRRMF.  Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account and a complete set of subsidiaries for the said funds.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose all the necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF.  Henceforth, strictly comply with the disclosure requirements on LDRRMF as provided by COA Circular No. 2014-002.
Management comment:
The Municipal Accountant made an additional disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements, particularly in Note 21, on the appropriation, utilization and the unexpended balance from CY 2012 – 2016.
Audit rejoinder:
The Municipal Accountant should also include disclosures on inventories and PPE acquisition and disposition from the LDRRMF.

We have recommended that the budget allocation of the municipality shall be at least 5% of the total budget appropriations as authorized under the General Appropriations Act to address gender issues and/or concerns of the locality.  The municipality shall submit GPBs to the PPDO of the Provincial Government to ensure the alignment of the municipal GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province and that the GAD Accomplishment Report and its accompanying documents shall be furnished to COA for audit as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
We have also recommended that the LGU shall establish its GFPS to ensure that GAD mechanisms and processes are in place; and the creation of GAD M&E Team who shall evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects in accordance with Item 5.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.  LGUs shall establish and/or strengthen their monitoring and evaluation systems to assess the effectiveness and efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts, and the M&E activities shall be included in the annual GPB.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding internal revenue taxes as mandated by laws, rules and regulations on all its future purchases of goods and payments for services for additional income of the government and to avoid possible imposition of sanctions by the BIR.

observations.managementAction: Management invited COA representative as witness in the conduct of the annual physical count of PPE for CY 2016, and submitted Report of Physical Count of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit the said DVs and its supporting documents in accordance with the provisions of Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 for evaluation/audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the said DVs with their respective supporting documents in September 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all financial accounts and reports shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit every 10th day of the following month.  Likewise, the Municipal Mayor should look into the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting Office to facilitate earlier submission of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to submit the year-end financial statements on February 13, 2017.  However, management was delayed in their submission of monthly and quarterly accounts and reports.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management justify the above-noted findings.  Henceforth, ensure that the amounts budgeted and disbursed for PS are within the limit as set forth by Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and DBM Local Budget Circular No. 98.  No additional appropriation and payments should be made for PS items in excess of the limit.
observations.managementAction: Share from Tobacco Excise Tax was not already included in the computation of the 55% PS limit appropriation for CY 2016 since no Share from Tobacco Excise was actually received by the municipality in CY 2014.  The total PS expense was within the PS limit appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that fully accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets be prepared to ensure proper use of government vehicle and consumption of fuel.  A summary of which shall be made at the end of each month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels and submit to this Office for audit/evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Driver’s Trip Tickets were submitted on the municipality’s fuel consumption for the months of July to December 2016.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.  Driver’s Trip Tickets were not submitted for the months of January to June 2016, and the submitted Driver’s Tickets for the months of July to December 2016 were not fully accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the guidelines set to process financial assistance should be observed to regulate the utilization of the fund.  The amount set for each type of financial assistance should be complied with to ascertain a non-discretionary determination of amounts given to each client.  We have also recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the noted lacking supporting documents in order to establish the validity of claims. The Municipal Accountant being responsible to the completeness of the supporting documents should not certify the disbursements unless all necessary documents are attached.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, documents required for each type of assistance were submitted.
observations.reason: Guideline is not yet set to ascertain a non-discretionary determination of amounts given to each client.  Also, RER or its equivalent properly signed by each client acknowledging receipt of the amount stated in the document should be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the agency officials explain the reasons for resorting to direct contracting, shopping and/or small value procurement as its methods of procurement for the above items with emphasis on the splitting of purchases.  We have also recommended that purchases be done through public bidding as mandated under Rule IV Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government of the Philippines is obtained.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.  Various transactions for CY 2016 were conducted through shopping, SVP and/or undetermined mode of procurement instead of public bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management justify the above-noted lapses.  Utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be limited on programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and yield greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We have also recommended that Management submit utilization reports for the KALAHI-CIDSS fund transfers for proper evaluation/audit.
observations.reason: Some expenditures made for CY 2016 partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management should comply strictly with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and the related subsequent provisions on LDRRMF utilization.   Personal services, maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRMO as well as the regular MOOE and capital outlay of the municipality shall be sourced from the General Fund.  The LDRRMF shall be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.  Some expenditures made for CY 2016 were properly charged against the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit to COA the JEV duly supported with valid documents in order to establish the reliability and accuracy of the journal entry made.  As such, we reiterate our previous recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should exert their best effort of locate the differences noted between their records by tracing back transaction in the previous year/s.
observations.reason: The JEV and supporting documents for the entry made in the TF not yet submitted.  Cashbook for the cash in bank as of December 31, 2016 was only submitted during the exit conference.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer should properly verify the Reports of Collections and Deposits.  He shall require that Part C (Accountability for Accountable Forms) of the RAAF be properly filled up.  He should check the Statements of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand and make a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors and check against ending balances on hand column.  Accountable forms should be issued only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections.
The Municipal Treasurer should prepare the CRAAF based on the verified RAAF and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for verification not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
The Municipal Accountant should install and maintain an effective internal control system on collections and deposits in order to safeguard and prevent possible loss of the accountable forms and government funds of the Municipality.
Valid justifications be submitted to explain the deficiencies noted on collections and the unaccounted/ missing accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: Report of Collections and Deposits were submitted for CY 2016.
observations.reason: Effective internal controls on collections and deposits were not yet fully implemented or observed by the collectors/treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant record purchases of supplies and materials to the respective inventory asset account and record these to their respective expenditure account upon issuance.  Purchases should be supported with Inspection and Acceptance Report.  All issuances should be supported with Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) should be prepared and submitted to COA to support charges made to expenditure accounts.  The Municipal Accountant should maintain Perpetual Records/ Supplies Ledger Cards per stock as provided by NGAS to ensure fair presentation of inventory accounts in the financial statements.   The Supply or Property Unit of the Municipal Treasurer as the case maybe, should maintain Stock Cards to record all receipts and issuances.
observations.reason: Purchase of some supplies and materials were recorded to their respective inventory account, however these were not supported with RIS and SSMI when they are record as expenses.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit the complete documentary requirements of paid Job order employees.  The hiring of job order employees shall likewise be authorized by a Sanggunian resolution.  Moreover, pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and R.A. No. 7160 on the documentation of Job order employees should be strictly complied.
observations.reason: SB Resolution, Contract and Accomplishment Reports were not submitted for job orders on infrastructure projects implemented by administration.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Government should only pay allowances, incentives and other benefits expressly authorized by the Sanggunian.  Government officials and employees should only be entitled to allowances, incentives and other benefits expressly provided by law, and other statutory authority, and the rules and regulations promulgated by competent authority.

We have also recommended that the MLGOO should refund the RATA payments made by the Municipal Government.  If not, the Local Chief Executive shall be held liable in accordance with the provisions of LBC No. 103.
observations.reason: Certification that the Devolved Government Official did not receive RATA from another LGU or to its mother agency was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare the status of appropriations, allotments and obligations for each fund to support the pre-closing trial balances and post-closing trial balances and schedule of subsidiary ledgers for each account and the necessary Registries pursuant to Sections 9 and 70, Chapter 1 and Chapter 2, Section 04(h), Vol. 1 of the NGAS in order to facilitate the verification of accounts appearing in the submitted financial statements.  Likewise, we have also recommended the Budget Officer for the maintenance of Registries to facilitate the monitoring of expenses and balances of appropriations and to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments.  Moreover, the agency should take into consideration the existence of appropriation before incurring expenditures in conformity with rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Some disbursements made for CY 2016 were in excess of appropriations.  Also, disbursement charged against the SEF amounting to P15,000.00 was made but not obligated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended strict adherence of the Municipality to the above-cited regulations that an appropriation ordinance or resolution for the utilization of the Municipality’s Share from Tobacco Excise Tax should be passed before entering into contract.  Management should submit an explanation for entering into contracts without the approved appropriation ordinance.
observations.reason: With regard to the 2016 Share from Tobacco Excise Tax utilization, a copy of the project proposal/s submitted to and approved by the DBM together with the SB resolution is requested to the municipality to be submitted to the Audit Team for audit/evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit the stated lacking documents for proper audit/ evaluation.  Management should submit the list of recipients/ beneficiaries (with their address and contact number for audit purposes) duly signed/ acknowledged, showing the items and quantity of items distributed to them.  Goods procured for stockpile/ disaster preparedness should not be immediately distributed rather these should be stored in a safe place, properly recorded/ accounted for and safeguarded, and should be distributed/ issued for its purpose or when needed.

We have also recommended that expenditures should be limited to what is included in the budget.  Management should carefully prepare the LDRRM Investment Plan, utilize and prioritize programs, projects and activities (PPAs) included in the plan to avoid over- and under-expenditure and partial/ non-implementation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Management said during the exit conference that the supplies and materials purchased in CY 2016 for relief operations were distributed after calamity, however they are still seeking the the signatures of the recipients/beneficiaries for submission.
observations.reason: As reiterated in this report, some expenditures charged against the LDRRMF for CY 2016 could not be appropriately evaluated if properly implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should remit immediately the amount due to the BIR and henceforth strictly comply on prompt remittance of taxes withheld.  The LGU should exert effort to help the BIR in its collection of taxes in as much as it is entirely dependent of its share from IRA.  The erring municipal officials concerned should be identified to answer personally the imposition and payment of the penalties, interest and surcharges.

It was likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer should, at all times, observe prudence in withholding and filing of tax returns.  The remittances on income taxes withheld should be compared with individual deductions of municipal officials and employees.  Summary of taxes withheld from suppliers/ contractors should be prepared at the end of each month.  The remittances on VAT and EWT withheld should be compared with the individual deductions of suppliers/ contractors.
observations.managementAction: Remittances were made monthly by the municipality.
observations.reason: Analysis of Due to BIR account disclosed that Due to BIR balance as of December 31, 2016 still includes the taxes withheld in the prior year/s up to December 2014.  Also some withholdings from January to November 2016 were not remitted as of December 31, 2016.
